name: Herrerasaurus
tagline: This old-timer was possibly one of the first meat-eating dinosaurs on Earth!
intro: One of the first dinosaurs of the Age of Dinosaurs, Herrerasaurus lived during the Late Triassic period, more than 200 million years ago. This small South American dinosaur was a very primitive, two-legged meat-eater about ten feet long. Herrerasaurus is classified as a lizard-hipped dinosaur, but shares only a few features with others in that group.

topics.title: At the Dawn of Dinosaurs
topics.text: Here are some of the dinosaurs that lived during the early periods of the Age of Dinosaurs.

captions.label: Plateosaurus
captions.text: This dinosaur is one of the most commonly found fossils. Plateosaurus was a plant-eater that grew up to twenty-three feet long.

captions.label: Staurikosaurus
captions.text: One of the oldest known dinosaurs, this little Brazilian predator lived during the Late Triassic period, 225 million years ago.

captions.label: Coelophysis
captions.text: Coelophysis dates from the Late Triassic period and has all the characteristics of a swift predator.

captions.label: Massospondylus
captions.text: This bulky plant-eater had a small head and a long neck and tail. It measured sixteen feet in length.
